channel.title: NDTV News Search Records Found 1000
channel.description: NDTV.com provides the latest information from and in-depth coverage of India and the world. Find breaking news, India news, top stories, elections, politics, business, cricket, movies, lifestyle, health, live TV, videos, photos and more.

item.title: Factbox: Latin America's 10 Deadliest Earthquakes

media:content.media:description: Until now, the deadliest quake in Venezuela's modern history was a 1967 event that left 240 people dead.

item.description: Two powerful earthquakes that struck the northern coast of Venezuela and capital Caracas on Wednesday claimed at least 188 lives, a toll that could increase as rescue teams comb the debris of collapsed buildings.
item.content:encoded: Two powerful earthquakes that struck the northern coast of Venezuela and capital Caracas on Wednesday claimed at least 188 lives, a toll that could increase as rescue teams comb the debris of collapsed buildings.

item.title: King Charles Will Not Live At Buckingham Palace After Renovations

media:content.media:description: Neither Charles nor the late Queen Elizabeth had stayed overnight at the palace since 2019.

item.description: King Charles will not live at Buckingham Palace after its 10-year refurbishment finishes next year, royal officials said on Thursday, ending nearly two centuries of the central London landmark serving as the British monarch's primary residence.
item.content:encoded: King Charles will not live at Buckingham Palace after its 10-year refurbishment finishes next year, royal officials said on Thursday, ending nearly two centuries of the central London landmark serving as the British monarch's primary residence.

item.title: Hezbollah Says Israel Killed 3 Civilians In Lebanon Amid US-Iran Talks

media:content.media:description: Hezbollah said it was the third "flagrant violation" by Israel "of the ceasefire.

item.description: Iran-backed Hezbollah on Thursday accused Israel of a "flagrant" ceasefire breach, saying it launched a drone and killed three civilians in southern Lebanon.
item.content:encoded: Iran-backed Hezbollah on Thursday accused Israel of a "flagrant" ceasefire breach, saying it launched a drone and killed three civilians in southern Lebanon.

item.title: Video: Iran President Left In The Sun? Shehbaz Sharif's 'Umbrella Moment' Goes Viral

media:content.media:description: The clip was recorded at Nur Khan Airbase and quickly spread across social media

item.description: It appears to show Pakistan Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif being protected from the sun as Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian and Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi walked beside him without an umbrella.
item.content:encoded: It appears to show Pakistan Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif being protected from the sun as Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian and Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi walked beside him without an umbrella.

item.title: Paris Bans Alcohol Consumption, Sales Amid Heatwave

media:content.media:description: French authorities said public consumption of alcohol would be banned in Paris from Friday.

item.description: Paris police told reporters that alcohol consumption in the streets and other public places as well as takeaway alcohol sales will be banned from Friday amid intense heatwave.
item.content:encoded: Paris police told reporters that alcohol consumption in the streets and other public places as well as takeaway alcohol sales will be banned from Friday amid intense heatwave.

item.title: 3.5 Lakh Haitians, 6,000 Syrians May Lose Deportation Protection In US

media:content.media:description: TPS protects its holders from deportation

item.description: The 6-3 ruling by the conservative-dominated court could have implications for more than one million beneficiaries of so-called Temporary Protected Status (TPS) from more than a dozen countries.
item.content:encoded: The 6-3 ruling by the conservative-dominated court could have implications for more than one million beneficiaries of so-called Temporary Protected Status (TPS) from more than a dozen countries.

item.title: Unresolved Rape Charge Against Harvey Weinstein Dropped

media:content.media:description: The Oscar-winning producer denies all the accusations.

item.description: Weinstein still stands convicted of another sexual felony in New York and others in California, and he remains behind bars.
item.content:encoded: Weinstein still stands convicted of another sexual felony in New York and others in California, and he remains behind bars.

item.title: Venezuela Earthquakes Live: At Least 188 Killed, 971 Injured

media:content.media:description: Venezuela Earthquake Live: The first earthquake had a magnitude of 7.2

item.description: 7.5 Magnitude Earthquake hit Venezuela Live Updates: The second earthquake struck just a minute later, with a 7.5-magnitude, and its epicentre was 16 kilometres southwest of Moron.
item.content:encoded: 7.5 Magnitude Earthquake hit Venezuela Live Updates: The second earthquake struck just a minute later, with a 7.5-magnitude, and its epicentre was 16 kilometres southwest of Moron.

item.title: Trump Administration Gets Supreme Court Approval On Stricter Immigration Rules

media:content.media:description: People who are eventually granted asylum can't be deported.

item.description: Under federal law, migrants who arrive in the US must be able to apply for asylum and be screened for fear of persecution in their home countries.
item.content:encoded: Under federal law, migrants who arrive in the US must be able to apply for asylum and be screened for fear of persecution in their home countries.

item.title: 105% Jump In Ship Crossings Through Hormuz, Iran Objects To Use Of Oman Route

media:content.media:description: IRGC has warned ships against using any route through Hormuz unless approved by Tehran

item.description: Data from maritime intelligence firm Kpler shows vessel crossings through the key waterway jumped 105 per cent on June 24, 2026, reaching 70 crossings.
item.content:encoded: Data from maritime intelligence firm Kpler shows vessel crossings through the key waterway jumped 105 per cent on June 24, 2026, reaching 70 crossings.

item.title: 40 Years After Air India Bombing Which Killed 329, Canada Admits It Was Khalistani Plot

media:content.media:description: The aircraft was on the way from Toronto to Mumbai when the explosion took place.

item.description: India's long-held position that the bombing was orchestrated by Khalistani extremists operating from Canada has been formally corroborated by Canadian authorities.
item.content:encoded: India's long-held position that the bombing was orchestrated by Khalistani extremists operating from Canada has been formally corroborated by Canadian authorities.

item.title: Unknown 'Projectile' Attacks Cargo Ship In Strait Of Hormuz

media:content.media:description: The incident follows more than a week of relative calm in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.description: British marine security firm Vanguard Tech identified that vessel as the Singapore-flagged container ship Ever Lovely.
item.content:encoded: British marine security firm Vanguard Tech identified that vessel as the Singapore-flagged container ship Ever Lovely.

item.title: Stolen Crypto, Secret Transfers: How Iran Moved $3.84 Billion Through CoinEx

media:content.media:description: Wallets connected to Iranian entities moved more than $3.84 billion through CoinEx

item.description: According to blockchain intelligence company TRM Labs, wallets connected to Iranian entities moved more than $3.84 billion through CoinEx since 2019.
item.content:encoded: According to blockchain intelligence company TRM Labs, wallets connected to Iranian entities moved more than $3.84 billion through CoinEx since 2019.

item.title: Gamers Relieved With Minimal Price Hike After Grand Theft Auto VI Preorder

media:content.media:description: Six years of development for "GTA VI" are estimated to have cost up to $2 billion.

item.description: Predicted to be the biggest-selling cultural product of all time, speculation had been rife that "GTA VI" would cost players far more than a typical high-end title.
item.content:encoded: Predicted to be the biggest-selling cultural product of all time, speculation had been rife that "GTA VI" would cost players far more than a typical high-end title.

item.title: PM Modi To Visit Seychelles From June 27-29, For National Day Celebrations

media:content.media:description: PM Modi last visited Seychelles in 2015

item.description: A contingent of the Indian armed forces, along with two ships of the Indian Navy, will participate in the celebrations, the Ministry of External Affairs said.
item.content:encoded: A contingent of the Indian armed forces, along with two ships of the Indian Navy, will participate in the celebrations, the Ministry of External Affairs said.

item.title: How Oil Tankers Crossed Hormuz Strait Despite Threats From Iran Guards

media:content.media:description: The oil tankers, led by the Stoic Warrior vessel, sailed along the coast of the United Arab Emirates.

item.description: The development comes as tensions rise between Iran and the United States over their interim accord aimed at permanently ending the Iran war.
item.content:encoded: The development comes as tensions rise between Iran and the United States over their interim accord aimed at permanently ending the Iran war.

item.title: "Jews Sick Of You": Book Reveals Trump's Outburst At Netanyahu In 2025 Call

media:content.media:description: The call took place in September 2025, during the United Nations General Assembly

item.description: The book, "Regime Change: Inside the Imperial Presidency of Donald Trump", talks about Trump's second term as president, during which the Republican leader brokered the ceasefire between Israel and Hamas.
item.content:encoded: The book, "Regime Change: Inside the Imperial Presidency of Donald Trump", talks about Trump's second term as president, during which the Republican leader brokered the ceasefire between Israel and Hamas.

item.title: Spain Records 212 Deaths Due To Heatwave In 4 Days: Report

media:content.media:description: Mainland Spain this week recorded its highest daily average temperatures in June since at least 1950.

item.description: The number of heat-related deaths in Spain between May 16 and September 30 last year hit 3,832, an 87.6-per cent increase from the same period in 2024.
item.content:encoded: The number of heat-related deaths in Spain between May 16 and September 30 last year hit 3,832, an 87.6-per cent increase from the same period in 2024.

item.title: Opinion: Opinion | Britain Seems To Have A New National Hobby: Firing Its Prime Ministers
item.description: Britain's Prime Ministers are dropping like mayflies, and nobody seems to be able to stop the mayhem.
item.content:encoded: Britain's Prime Ministers are dropping like mayflies, and nobody seems to be able to stop the mayhem.

item.title: How To Live A Long Life? Scientists Tap Brazilian Sisters Aged 103, 104, 109

media:content.media:description: The three sisters credit their longevity to a healthy diet and an active lifestyle.

item.description: Findings from the three sisters' case could help scientists better understand why some people remain physically and cognitively resilient at exceptionally advanced ages.
item.content:encoded: Findings from the three sisters' case could help scientists better understand why some people remain physically and cognitively resilient at exceptionally advanced ages.
